rows.eraNote: Twin-turbo N47 — rear-chain risk; EGR cooler recall. Watch for cold-start rattle.

buyingChecks.title: Buying This 125d? Check These First
buyIf: * Post-2015 facelift with the B47 engine (significantly reduced chain risk)
* Documented timing chain history on any N47-engined car
* Full service history with regular oil changes (critical for chain longevity)
avoidIf: * Pre-2015 N47 with cold-start rattle and no chain history
* No service history — the N47 is unforgiving of neglect
* Short-journey ownership history — DPF issues likely
* Unexplained coolant loss — EGR cooler may be failing

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The 125d is BMW's twin-turbo 2.0-litre diesel — 218 bhp in the N47S1 pre-facelift, with the B47 offering similar power but significantly improved reliability. The N47's rear-mounted timing chain is the defining risk of the earlier cars; the B47's front-mounted chain is a genuine improvement. With typical F20 values of £5,000–£15,000, a £4,000–£5,500 engine replacement is borderline on lower-value examples but often worth it for a well-maintained car.

cta.label: → Compare 125d engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used BMW 1 Series 125d (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * Cold Start — Listen for 1–30 seconds of rattle (N47 timing chain warning) — see the map above
* Service History — Including which engine era (N47S1 or B47) and oil-change discipline
* EGR Recall — Confirm recall completion with BMW (both N47 and B47 affected)
* Smoke Check — White smoke on startup can indicate EGR coolant leak
* DPF Regeneration — Check history; short journeys accelerate wear on both diesel eras
* Turbo Boost — Test acceleration; any lag, whistling, or grinding? (twin-turbo setup)
* Engine Warning Light — Have codes been read? Any unresolved issues?
* MOT History — Check for emissions-related failures

items.question: How much does a BMW 1 Series 125d engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: A reconditioned 125d engine typically costs £3,200–£5,500 supply only, with the B47 variant at the higher end. Fitting adds £800–£1,300 depending on the specialist. Used engines can be found from £1,250–£2,500, but condition varies dramatically.

items.question: Is the BMW 1 Series 125d engine reliable?
items.answer: It depends entirely on which engine era your 125d has. The pre-facelift N47S1 (2012–2015) has the well-documented rear-mounted timing chain risk — a known weakness that can cause catastrophic failure. The facelift B47 (2015–2019) is significantly more reliable — the chain was relocated to the front, fixing the N47's timing-chain issues. Both engines share EGR cooler issues, subject to a BMW recall.

items.question: What are the common problems with the BMW 1 Series 125d?
items.answer: Timing chain wear on N47-engined cars (cold-start rattle is the warning sign), EGR cooler leaks affecting both N47 and B47 (subject to a BMW recall), and DPF clogging exacerbated by short journeys. The B47 is a significant improvement over the N47.

items.question: Is it worth replacing a 125d engine, or should I scrap the car?
items.answer: Depends on vehicle value and engine era. Against a well-kept £7,000–£10,000 F20 125d, a £4,000–£5,000 recon is 40–70% of the car's value — borderline but often worth it for a cherished example. Against a tired £4,000 example, the same recon may exceed our 70% BMW threshold — get a scrap valuation first. B47-engined cars command a premium due to improved reliability.

items.question: How do I find my BMW 1 Series 125d engine code?
items.answer: Pre-facelift (2012–2015) 125d engines carry the N47D20D (N47S1) code. Facelift (2015–2019) cars use the B47D20B. Check the VIN plate or vehicle documentation. The facelift was introduced in 2015 — if your car is registered after mid-2015, it's almost certainly the B47.

items.question: Can I repair the N47 timing chain, or does it need replacement?
items.answer: Yes — if caught early. A preventative chain replacement (£650–£1,800) is far cheaper than a catastrophic chain snap (£3,200–£5,500 engine replacement). At the first sign of cold-start rattle, book an inspection immediately. The B47 has far fewer timing chain issues — but it's still worth monitoring.
